title: WolfHsmError::check uses numeric range for WH_ERROR_* classification
description: error.rs classifies return codes in -2302..=-2000 as Wh errors and anything else nonzero as Ffi errors. The range directly mirrors wolfHSM WH_ERROR_* header constants without re-exporting them. This is intentional: the range is stable, embedding it makes check() a one-liner, and the doc comment records it for header verification. A future contributor might suggest matching against exported constants instead, but that would pull all WH_ERROR_* symbols into Rust and add noise without correctness gain.
